decisions.reasoning: Russia GDP growth divergence: WB 4.3% (likely 2024 actual, military spending boom) vs IMF 0.6% (Oct 2025 forecast). WB data more current. Both are credible for different time periods.
Argentina GDP: WB -1.3% (2024 recession under Milei shock therapy) vs IMF 4.5% (2025 recovery forecast). Different reference years explain divergence. WB actual data preferred.
Ireland GDP volatility is structural (multinational accounting, 'leprechaun economics'). WB 2.6% vs IMF 9.1%. Large swings are normal for Ireland. WB actual data preferred.
Iran GDP: WB 3.7% (pre-war 2024 data) vs IMF 0.6%. Both are pre-war figures. The current US-Iran war (Feb 2026) will significantly impact future GDP. WB data is more recent.
Kuwait GDP: WB -2.6% (OPEC+ production cuts impact) vs IMF 2.6%. Oil-dependent economy sensitive to production quotas. WB actual data preferred.
Libya GDP: WB 1.9% vs IMF 15.6%. Libya's GDP is extremely volatile due to oil production disruptions. 15.6% was post-disruption recovery; 1.9% is normalization. WB data is more current.
